Verify eNABLE_BM25=true activates the in-memory BM25 path; trigger phrase edit triggers BM25 re-index; new trigger is searchable after re-index; old trigger phrase still works if not removed; FTS5 remains available as the default lexical path when BM25 is off. Return a concise pass/fail verdict with the main reason and cited evidence.

As a retrieval validation operator, validate BM25 trigger phrase re-index gate against ENABLE_BM25=true. Verify trigger edit causes re-index when BM25 is enabled. Return a concise pass/fail verdict with the main reason and cited evidence.
expectedSignals: `ENABLE_BM25=true` activates the in-memory BM25 path
trigger phrase edit triggers BM25 re-index
new trigger is searchable after re-index
old trigger phrase still works if not removed
FTS5 remains available as the default lexical path when BM25 is off
unmatchedSignals: `ENABLE_BM25=true` activates the in-memory BM25 path
trigger phrase edit triggers BM25 re-index
new trigger is searchable after re-index
old trigger phrase still works if not removed

evidence:
Command sequence is not expressible as direct handler calls: 1. Start with `ENABLE_BM25=true` and verify `isBm25Enabled()` activates the in-memory BM25 path
2. Edit trigger phrases and confirm the update handler re-index condition still checks title OR trigger phrases when BM25 is enabled
